examples: During the turmoil in the foreign-exchange markets the guilder remained strong.
The striking, yellow 50 guilder note features a sunflower prominently.
The Dutch guilder was retired in 2002, when it was replaced by the Euro.
The Dutch guilder and the D-mark were sacrificed on the altar of European unification.
The guilder dates back to the 13th Century, making it one of the oldest currencies in Europe.
